source_description: Time-tested-validation conflicts with red-queen-effect when time-tested solutions are outpaced by competitive adaptation that makes historical validation obsolete.
source_quote: time-tested-validation conflicts with red-queen-effect (Red Queen effect) when time-tested solutions are outpaced by competitive adaptation that makes historical validation obsolete.

source_description: Leverage-points gives tipping-points a concrete systems lens for locating where a small intervention can unlock disproportionate change.
source_quote: Leverage points are places to intervene in a system where minimum effort can lead to maximum possible change.
affinity_rationale: Tipping points are fundamentally about identifying high-leverage intervention points where small changes trigger disproportionate effects
activation_condition: When identifying where small interventions will trigger disproportionate systemic change

source_description: First-principles-thinking helps tipping-points strip away default assumptions so the real threshold condition can be seen and tested.
source_quote: By stripping away conventional assumptions ... this method facilitates the breakthrough novel solutions needed to find a non-obvious tipping point.
affinity_rationale: Canonical states FPT facilitates breakthrough solutions needed to find non-obvious tipping points
activation_condition: When finding non-obvious tipping points requires stripping away conventional assumptions

source_description: Optimism-bias-and-planning-fallacy undermines tipping-points by causing teams to overestimate the probability that each sequential step will succeed, masking how far the system actually is from the threshold.
source_quote: When a project requires many sequential steps to succeed, people tend to overestimate the overall probability of success. The successful completion of a new product development, for example, is conjunctive, meaning that even if each step is very likely, the overall probability of success can be low if the number of events is large.
affinity_rationale: Conjunctive bias causes overestimation of success probability when achieving a tipping point requires many sequential steps
activation_condition: When overestimating probability of achieving planned tipping point due to conjunctive bias

source_description: Tipping-points conflicts with status-quo-bias when slow drift is misread as stable equilibrium near a threshold.
source_quote: tipping-points vs status-quo-bias: tipping-points conflicts with status-quo-bias (status quo bias) when slow drift is misread as stable equilibrium near threshold.

source_description: Tipping-points conflicts with representativeness-heuristic when recent normal patterns obscure an approaching regime shift.
source_quote: tipping-points vs representativeness-heuristic: tipping-points conflicts with representativeness-heuristic (representativeness heuristic) when recent normal patterns obscure approaching regime shift.

source_description: Tipping-points conflicts with confirmation-bias when teams force incoming signals to fit the expected threshold story instead of testing whether the shift is real.
source_quote: tipping-points vs confirmation-bias: tipping-points conflicts with confirmation-bias (confirmation bias) when prediction errors and misread signals are filtered to fit the expected threshold narrative rather than honestly assessed.

source_description: Prioritization helps trade-offs turn constrained choice into explicit ranking instead of rhetorical balance.
source_quote: Prioritization and Focus
affinity_rationale: Trade-off decisions require systematic ranking and resource allocation among competing alternatives
activation_condition: When focusing resources requires systematic ranking of competing alternatives

source_description: Opportunity-cost sharpens trade-offs by making the hidden cost of saying yes visible.
source_quote: Best when decision quality depends on naming the hidden cost of saying yes
affinity_rationale: Every trade-off implies a foregone alternative; opportunity cost is the intrinsic mechanism of trade-offs
activation_condition: When every choice implies a foregone alternative that must be explicitly valued

source_description: Systems-thinking helps trade-offs reveal the interrelationships and feedback loops that make one compromise carry costs elsewhere.
source_quote: This framework enables the visualization of interrelationships and feedback loops, revealing the causal links that necessitate trade-offs.
affinity_rationale: Canonical describes systems thinking as enabling visualization of feedback loops that reveal causal links necessitating trade-offs
activation_condition: When visualizing feedback loops reveals the causal links that necessitate trade-offs

source_description: Anchoring undermines trade-offs by over-weighting the first frame or metric and obscuring better reallocations.
source_quote: This cognitive bias causes over-focus on a single piece of information, preventing objective analysis of trade-offs.
affinity_rationale: Canonical states anchoring causes over-focus on a single piece of information, preventing objective trade-off analysis
activation_condition: When over-focus on a single data point prevents objective analysis of alternatives

source_description: Trade-offs conflicts with status-quo-bias when hidden opportunity costs are ignored to preserve defaults.
source_quote: trade-offs vs status-quo-bias: trade-offs conflicts with status-quo-bias (status quo bias) when hidden opportunity costs are ignored to preserve defaults.

source_description: Trade-offs conflicts with commitment-bias when legacy priorities block reallocation after new evidence.
source_quote: trade-offs vs commitment-bias: trade-offs conflicts with commitment-bias (commitment bias) when legacy priorities block reallocation after new evidence.

source_description: Einstellung-effect undermines tradition-vs-innovation-balance by causing a currently held idea or strategy to block consideration of fresh alternatives, collapsing the balance toward tradition.
source_quote: Einstellung (Mental Set): The psychological phenomenon where an idea or strategy currently held in mind (tradition/expertise) blocks the consideration of fresh thoughts, preventing creative breakthroughs.
affinity_rationale: Canonical names Einstellung as blocking consideration of innovative alternatives when existing mental sets dominate
activation_condition: When existing mental sets and expertise block consideration of innovative alternatives

source_description: Tradition-vs-innovation-balance conflicts with status-quo-bias when preference for the current state prevents needed adaptation.
source_quote: tradition-vs-innovation-balance conflicts with status-quo-bias (status quo bias) when status Quo Bias emphasizes the preference for existing conditions, which can resist the innovative changes needed in the Tradition Vs Innovation Balance.

source_description: Tradition-vs-innovation-balance conflicts with commitment-bias when prior commitments harden into overreliance on old methods.
source_quote: tradition-vs-innovation-balance conflicts with commitment-bias (commitment and consistency bias) when commitment and Consistency Bias can lead to an over-reliance on traditional methods, hindering the flexibility required for innovation.

source_description: Tradition-vs-innovation-balance conflicts with confirmation-bias when teams keep collecting support for tradition while ignoring evidence that the balance point should shift.
source_quote: tradition-vs-innovation-balance conflicts with confirmation-bias (confirmation bias) when teams seek evidence that confirms the existing tradition while dismissing signals that the balance point needs to shift.

source_description: Risk-vs-uncertainty helps true-uncertainty-navigation keep robust action tied to how much of the uncertainty is actually reducible.
source_quote: uncertainty is materially affecting capital allocation, sequencing, or commitment decisions
affinity_rationale: Risk-vs-uncertainty distinction is foundational; canonical describes five-level uncertainty schema for navigating ambiguity
activation_condition: When distinguishing between quantifiable risk and true uncertainty determines which analytical tools apply

source_description: Step-back helps true-uncertainty-navigation avoid panic or premature commitment by forcing a wider look before choosing the next action.
source_quote: put your plan aside and focus elsewhere for a while
affinity_rationale: Canonical emphasizes metacognitive monitoring and problem reframing, which stepping back enables
activation_condition: When reflective pause enables metacognitive assessment of the problem framing under uncertain conditions

source_description: Confirmation-bias makes true-uncertainty-navigation dishonest by adjusting uncertainty estimates to protect the expected answer.
source_quote: the mind is biased to just accept and applaud confirming evidence
affinity_rationale: Canonical structured tension identifies confirmation bias as adjusting uncertainty estimates to confirm expected outcomes
activation_condition: When uncertainty estimates are adjusted to confirm expected outcomes rather than honestly reflecting information limits

source_description: True-uncertainty-navigation conflicts with commitment-bias when teams over-commit before uncertainty is reduced.
source_quote: true-uncertainty-navigation vs commitment-bias: true-uncertainty-navigation conflicts with commitment-bias when teams over-commit before uncertainty is reduced.

source_description: True-uncertainty-navigation conflicts with representativeness-heuristic when familiar narratives are mistaken for reliable priors.
source_quote: true-uncertainty-navigation vs representativeness-heuristic: true-uncertainty-navigation conflicts with representativeness-heuristic when familiar narratives are mistaken for reliable priors.

source_description: True-uncertainty-navigation conflicts with confirmation-bias when uncertainty estimates are adjusted to confirm the expected outcome rather than honestly reflecting the limits of available information.
source_quote: true-uncertainty-navigation vs confirmation-bias: true-uncertainty-navigation conflicts with confirmation-bias when uncertainty estimates are adjusted to confirm the expected outcome rather than honestly reflecting the limits of available information.

source_description: Systems-thinking helps understanding-motivations trace behavior back to organizational design, role structure, and coordinated incentives rather than to personality stories alone.
source_quote: Thinking of an organization as an evolutionary machine designed to achieve a goal requires a focus on motivation.
affinity_rationale: Canonical describes organizational design as a machine requiring coordination of people with aligned motivations and goals
activation_condition: When organizational design requires aligning roles, goals, and individual strengths to motivational structures

source_description: Pareto-principle helps understanding-motivations focus on the few motive-linked actions that drive most of the observed result.
source_quote: The Pareto Principle ... Motivation clarifies which 20% to focus on
affinity_rationale: Canonical states understanding motivations guides which 20% of actions to focus on for 80% of results
activation_condition: When identifying the 20% of actions that align with core values drives 80% of motivated outcomes

source_description: Confirmation-bias distorts understanding-motivations when analysts read the hidden drivers they already expect instead of what the evidence supports.
source_quote: Confirmation Bias / Groupthink
affinity_rationale: Canonical structured tension identifies selective surfacing of drivers that confirm preferred interventions
activation_condition: When motivational analysis selectively surfaces drivers that confirm the analyst's preferred intervention

source_description: Constraints checks understanding-motivations when a behavior that looks deeply psychological is actually driven by simpler situational limits.
source_quote: Danger when analysts become so committed to hidden-motive interpretation that they over-psychologize simple constraint or capability problems.
affinity_rationale: Constraints can limit the scope of motivational analysis, but the canonical does not directly name this relationship
activation_condition: When resource or structural limitations prevent full exploration of motivational landscape

source_description: Understanding-motivations conflicts with simplification when mapping the full motivational landscape adds complexity beyond what a simple incentive lever would require.
source_quote: understanding-motivations conflicts with simplification (simplification) when mapping the full motivational landscape adds complexity beyond what a simple incentive lever would require.

source_description: Understanding-motivations conflicts with confirmation-bias when motivational analysis selectively surfaces drivers that confirm the analyst's preferred intervention.
source_quote: understanding-motivations conflicts with confirmation-bias (confirmation bias) when motivational analysis selectively surfaces drivers that confirm the analyst's preferred intervention.

source_description: Cognitive-load-theory helps usability-heuristics reduce working-memory strain by chunking tasks and simplifying interface or process demands.
source_quote: CLT reminds us that working memory is a limited resource.
affinity_rationale: Canonical states heuristics and CLT work synergistically, as heuristics directly reduce cognitive load on limited working memory
activation_condition: When heuristic design patterns reduce working memory burden during user interactions

source_description: Problem-framing-and-reframing helps usability-heuristics act as a first cut on the problem instead of a frozen answer to it.
source_quote: cleave problems into insightful parts
affinity_rationale: Canonical describes heuristics as integral to initial hypothesis generation and problem-solving framing
activation_condition: When reframing usability problems through different lenses reveals overlooked interaction patterns

source_description: First-principles-thinking resists usability-heuristics when the shortcut masks a genuinely novel problem that needs fresh reconstruction from facts.
source_quote: First Principles Thinking strips away assumptions and conventional methods.
affinity_rationale: Canonical states heuristics involve applying existing patterns while FPT strips away assumptions for novel solutions
activation_condition: When applying established usability patterns prevents novel interface solutions that first-principles design would generate

source_description: Usability-heuristics conflicts with first-principles-thinking when applying established usability patterns prevents the novel interface solutions that first-principles design would generate.
source_quote: usability-heuristics conflicts with first-principles-thinking (first principles thinking) when applying established usability patterns prevents the novel interface solutions that first-principles design would generate.

source_description: Usability-heuristics conflicts with optimization-theory when usability satisficing settles for good-enough interaction when optimization would yield measurably superior outcomes.
source_quote: usability-heuristics conflicts with optimization-theory (optimization theory) when usability satisficing settles for good-enough interaction when optimization would yield measurably superior outcomes.

source_description: Empathy gives user-centered-design the perspective-taking discipline needed to understand user fears, goals, and contradictions before solutioning.
source_quote: Both approaches emphasize empathy as foundational.
affinity_rationale: Empathy is the foundational entry point of UCD; the Empathize phase requires deep engagement with user perspectives
activation_condition: When understanding user needs requires deep engagement with their perspective, emotions, and context

source_description: Experimentation helps user-centered-design turn prototypes into evidence instead of design intuition or advocacy.
source_quote: iterative and complementary
affinity_rationale: Canonical describes UCD's iterative build-test cycle for validating design decisions against real user behavior
activation_condition: When iterative prototyping and testing validates design decisions against real user behavior

source_description: Confirmation-bias undermines user-centered-design when observation and testing are quietly steered to protect the design story the team already prefers.
source_quote: willingness to suppress assumptions
affinity_rationale: Canonical warns teams suppress skepticism under groupthink rather than letting user research challenge assumptions
activation_condition: When teams suppress skepticism in favor of prevailing design vision rather than letting user research challenge assumptions

source_description: User-centered-design conflicts with authority-bias when internal rank or expert status outweighs direct user evidence.
source_quote: authority-bias

source_description: User-centered-design conflicts with confirmation-bias when discovery and testing are used to validate a preferred concept instead of discovering what users actually need.
source_quote: suppress assumptions

source_description: Empathy helps user-experience-research-methods interpret what people say, do, feel, and avoid without flattening it into internal product language.
source_quote: deep personal understanding
affinity_rationale: User experience research is fundamentally empathy research, capturing emotional and experiential dimensions of user interaction
activation_condition: When research methods must capture the emotional and experiential dimensions of user interaction

source_description: Systems-thinking helps user-experience-research-methods treat journeys and interactions as connected system behavior rather than isolated moments.
source_quote: A user journey map, for instance, is a systems map.
affinity_rationale: Canonical states UXRM is deeply complementary to systems thinking for mapping interrelationships across touchpoints
activation_condition: When mapping user journeys requires understanding systemic interrelationships across touchpoints

source_description: Confirmation-bias turns user-experience-research-methods into interview theater by using research to validate an answer that leadership already prefers.
source_quote: when interview design seeks validation instead of discovery
affinity_rationale: Canonical structured tension identifies interview design seeking validation instead of discovery
activation_condition: When interview design seeks validation of existing assumptions instead of genuine discovery

source_description: User-experience-research-methods conflicts with confirmation-bias when interview design seeks validation instead of discovery.
source_quote: user-experience-research-methods vs confirmation-bias: user-experience-research-methods conflicts with confirmation-bias when interview design seeks validation instead of discovery.

source_description: User-experience-research-methods conflicts with authority-bias when stakeholder rank outweighs user evidence.
source_quote: user-experience-research-methods vs authority-bias: user-experience-research-methods conflicts with authority-bias when stakeholder rank outweighs user evidence.

source_description: Experimentation gives variation-and-selection the real-world testing discipline needed to separate promising variants from weak ones.
source_quote: Treating solutions as a portfolio of experiments allows real-world data to steer the subsequent direction.
affinity_rationale: Variation-and-selection IS experimentation; generating multiple approaches maximizes discovery probability
activation_condition: When generating and testing multiple approaches maximizes the probability of finding optimal solutions

source_description: Base-rates keep variation-and-selection from choosing attractive variants that collapse once the selection step is checked against reference-class reality.
source_quote: The Outside View provides the objective standard necessary for the Selection phase.
affinity_rationale: Canonical describes the outside view as providing the objective standard necessary for the selection phase
activation_condition: When reference-class data provides the objective standard for evaluating which variations to select

source_description: Curse-of-knowledge undermines variation-and-selection by causing experts to leap directly to an overarching principle, skipping the divergent generation of concrete building blocks the model requires.
source_quote: The Curse of Knowledge: Experts suffer from the inability to recall what it was like not to know something. Their expertise often causes them to immediately jump to the 'answer' or overarching principle (a form of premature Selection). This inhibits the necessary Variation needed to simplify ideas into the concrete, elementary building blocks required for effective communication or teaching.
affinity_rationale: Canonical states expertise causes premature selection by jumping to answers, inhibiting necessary variation
activation_condition: When expertise causes premature selection by jumping to familiar answers, inhibiting necessary variation

source_description: Variation-and-selection conflicts with status-quo-bias when exploration of new options is blocked by attachment to the current solution.
source_quote: variation-and-selection vs status-quo-bias: variation-and-selection conflicts with status-quo-bias (status quo bias) when variation and Selection challenges the Status Quo Bias by encouraging exploration of new options rather than sticking to existing solutions.

source_description: Variation-and-selection conflicts with commitment-bias when early commitments stop the team from generating or fairly selecting better alternatives.
source_quote: variation-and-selection vs commitment-bias: variation-and-selection conflicts with commitment-bias (commitment and consistency bias) when commitment and Consistency Bias can hinder the Variation phase by discouraging deviation from initial choices, opposing the model's emphasis on exploring diverse options.

source_description: Variation-and-selection conflicts with confirmation-bias when the search space is narrowed to options that merely confirm the current belief.
source_quote: variation-and-selection vs confirmation-bias: variation-and-selection conflicts with confirmation-bias (confirmation bias) when variation search is constrained to options that confirm existing beliefs, preventing the model from discovering superior alternatives.

source_description: Systems-thinking strengthens varied-practice-interleaving by helping teams compare frames around interrelationships and patterns of change instead of isolated facts.
source_quote: In systems thinking, VPI helps navigate complexity by recognizing that complex systems cannot be understood by merely accumulating isolated facts.
affinity_rationale: Interleaving diverse frameworks supports the development of systemic understanding across domains
activation_condition: When interleaving diverse frameworks builds capacity for seeing interrelationships across domains

source_description: Feynman-technique helps varied-practice-interleaving turn frame contrast into deeper understanding by forcing explanation through multiple analogies and simple language.
source_quote: The crucial final step is to use an analogy to explain the concept, which is a key component of VPI.
affinity_rationale: Canonical names the Feynman Technique as using analogy to test depth of understanding, a key VPI ally
activation_condition: When using analogy and simplification tests depth of understanding across varied practice domains

source_description: Einstellung-effect undermines varied-practice-interleaving by causing rapid pattern recognition to lock in the first frame, preventing the genuine frame rotation the model demands.
source_quote: Expertise relies on rapid pattern recognition for efficiency. VPI disrupts this reliance, forcing the use of slower, more analytical thinking when the initial pattern recognized is potentially misleading.
affinity_rationale: Canonical describes expertise and pattern recognition fixation as disrupted by VPI's requirement for varied analysis
activation_condition: When expertise-driven pattern recognition fixation prevents genuine engagement with varied practice

source_description: Varied-practice-interleaving conflicts with anchoring when early information locks the team into the first frame instead of allowing genuine frame contrast.
source_quote: varied-practice-interleaving vs anchoring: varied-practice-interleaving conflicts with anchoring (anchoring) when anchoring can limit the flexibility needed in varied practice interleaving by causing reliance on initial information or perspectives.

source_description: Varied-practice-interleaving conflicts with confirmation-bias when teams choose frames that confirm the current hypothesis instead of exposing it to a real alternative.
source_quote: varied-practice-interleaving vs confirmation-bias: varied-practice-interleaving conflicts with confirmation-bias (confirmation bias) when motivated reasoning and inside-view bias cause practitioners to select frames that confirm their existing hypothesis rather than genuinely contrasting alternatives that would expose it.

source_description: Confirmation bias amplifies WYSIATI — not only does available evidence feel complete, but the search for more evidence is also biased toward confirming the story already constructed; WYSIATI closes the picture while confirmation bias reinforces it.
source_quote: Confirmation bias amplifies WYSIATI: not only does the available evidence feel complete, but the search for more evidence is also biased toward confirming the story already constructed.
affinity_rationale: Canonical states confirmation bias amplifies WYSIATI by biasing evidence search toward confirming the already-constructed story
activation_condition: When the search for additional evidence is biased toward confirming the story already constructed from available information

source_description: The first piece of evidence encountered anchors the subsequent story-building that WYSIATI performs — the anchor is not just a reference point, it is the seed from which the entire coherent narrative grows.
source_quote: The first piece of evidence encountered anchors the subsequent story-building that WYSIATI performs.
affinity_rationale: Canonical describes the anchor as the seed from which the entire coherent WYSIATI narrative grows
activation_condition: When the first piece of evidence seeds the coherent narrative that WYSIATI then closes around

source_description: Survivorship bias produces the structured missing denominator that WYSIATI then fails to register — the survivors are in the picture, the failures are absent, and WYSIATI reports the picture as complete.
source_quote: Survivorship bias produces the structured missing denominator that WYSIATI then fails to register.
affinity_rationale: Canonical states survivorship bias produces the structured missing denominator that WYSIATI fails to register
activation_condition: When absence of failure data produces the structured missing denominator that WYSIATI fails to register

source_description: Base rates provide the outside-view evidence that WYSIATI excludes — the distributional facts about the reference class that are not in the immediate picture.
source_quote: Base rates provide the outside-view evidence that WYSIATI excludes: the distributional facts about the reference class not in the immediate picture.
affinity_rationale: Canonical states base rates force introduction of outside-view evidence that WYSIATI has excluded
activation_condition: When outside-view reference-class data must be forced into a picture that WYSIATI has already closed

source_description: Critical thinking's discipline of evidence quality assessment and assumption surfacing directly fights WYSIATI by inserting the 'what is missing?' question before conclusions are drawn.
source_quote: Critical thinking fights WYSIATI by inserting the 'what is missing?' question before conclusions are drawn.
affinity_rationale: Canonical states critical thinking directly fights WYSIATI through evidence quality assessment and assumption surfacing
activation_condition: When evidence quality assessment and assumption surfacing must override the fluent coherence of available information

source_description: WYSIATI conflicts with base-rates when the mind's closure around available evidence prevents the outside-view reference-class data from entering the picture at all.
source_quote: WYSIATI vs base-rates: WYSIATI conflicts with base-rates (base rates) when the mind's closure around available evidence prevents the outside-view reference-class data from entering the picture at all.

source_description: WYSIATI conflicts with information-asymmetry when the mind treats currently-available information as complete, suppressing the recognition that the other party has material information absent from the current picture.
source_quote: WYSIATI vs information-asymmetry: WYSIATI conflicts with information-asymmetry (information asymmetry) when the mind treats currently-available information as complete.

source_description: WYSIATI conflicts with probabilistic-thinking when narrative coherence generates overconfidence in conclusions drawn from incomplete evidence, collapsing what should be a distribution into a single confident point estimate.
source_quote: WYSIATI vs probabilistic-thinking: WYSIATI conflicts with probabilistic-thinking (probabilistic thinking) when narrative coherence generates overconfidence in conclusions drawn from incomplete evidence.

source_description: WYSIATI conflicts with critical-thinking when the fluency and internal coherence of the available story suppresses the signal that scrutiny, gap-identification, and assumption-testing are needed.
source_quote: WYSIATI vs critical-thinking: WYSIATI conflicts with critical-thinking (critical thinking) when the fluency and internal coherence of the available story suppresses the signal that scrutiny is needed.

source_description: Scaffolding gives zone-of-development its practical mechanism by shaping the next reachable step instead of demanding mastery in one leap.
source_quote: The scaffolding process is a truly transferable skill applicable in nearly any domain.
affinity_rationale: Canonical names scaffolding as the primary mechanism of ZOD, breaking problems into parts and providing structured incremental support
activation_condition: When structured, incremental support enables learners to bridge from current competence to the next development level

source_description: Systems-thinking is the long-run payoff of zone-of-development because structured learning of parts eventually reveals interrelationships and the big picture.
source_quote: The ZOD model provides the mechanism for acquiring systems thinking.
affinity_rationale: Canonical lists scaffolding and systems thinking together as the first ally, with ZOD developing capacity for systemic understanding
activation_condition: When ZOD progression develops the capacity to see interrelationships and patterns of change

source_description: Expertise-reversal-effect undermines zone-of-development by causing experts to skip calibration of challenge to the learner's actual level, communicating at abstractions the learner cannot yet use.
source_quote: The Illusion of Understanding (Expert Trap): Expertise can create an illusion of understanding or lead to overconfidence, resulting in an unwillingness to investigate or simplify. Experts often struggle to communicate simply because they naturally want to speak on a higher level of abstraction, leading to the Curse of Knowledge where the message is accurate but useless to the novice.
affinity_rationale: Expertise reversal occurs when scaffolding structures designed for novices become counterproductive for advanced learners
activation_condition: When scaffolding structures become counterproductive for learners who have already internalized the material

source_description: Zone-of-development conflicts with status-quo-bias when comfort-zone routines replace stretch-learning progression.
source_quote: zone-of-development vs status-quo-bias: zone-of-development conflicts with status-quo-bias (status quo bias) when comfort-zone routines replace stretch-learning progression.

source_description: Zone-of-development conflicts with authority-bias when rank-based assumptions about instruction block the learner from getting the right growth challenge.
source_quote: zone-of-development vs authority-bias: zone-of-development conflicts with authority-bias (authority bias) when learner growth is constrained by rank-based instruction assumptions.
